body.title: [triage] superclaude.ps1/.bat carry pre-merge 1M-default/no-delegation behavior — reconcile to 200K + rescueclaude delegation
body.intention: I intend that the Windows launcher variants match the bash launchers' contract (200K default, single rescueclaude chokepoint), so a Windows launch does not silently diverge on context-window mode or the OpenRouter strip.
body.description:
The bash launchers were merged (superclaude delegates to rescueclaude, the single chokepoint; default flipped 1M->200K; OpenRouter-var strip moved into rescueclaude). The Windows variants were NOT touched (Linux-only that session) and still carry the old behavior: bin/superclaude.ps1, superclaude.ps1 (repo root), bin/superclaude.bat.

**Proposed shape:** mirror the bash design on Windows — 200K default with --1m opt-in, and (if a rescueclaude.ps1 is warranted) a single launch chokepoint; at minimum flip the default and ensure the OpenRouter routing vars are stripped before invoking claude.
**Affected files:** bin/superclaude.ps1, superclaude.ps1, bin/superclaude.bat.
**Scope boundary:** IN -- reconcile the Windows variants to the merged bash contract. OUT -- the bash launchers (done).
**Test plan:** on Windows (or pwsh), a bare launch reports/uses 200K, --1m opts into 1M, and the claude invocation has no ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL/AUTH_TOKEN/API_KEY leaking.
**Completion criteria:** Windows variants default to 200K + strip the OpenRouter vars, matching bin/rescueclaude.

Related: bin/rescueclaude, bin/superclaude, incident_superclaude_401, commit 24151630.

[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)

[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
